title: Issue 2239 terminal outcome custody

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Close #2239 by making closeout custody explicit: DELIVERED, BLOCKED, USER_PAUSED, and CONTINUE are projected from completion gates, and CONTINUE forbids terminal final-response claims while safe in-scope continuation remains.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not treat progress, context pressure, task size, successful compaction, or one completed slice as terminal blockers; BLOCKED requires an external or human-action blocker with no safe in-scope continuation.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Exact projection shape, compact report fields, and focused regression coverage for closeout_trust, closeout_claim_boundary, closeout_report, and final-response rendering.
canonical_core.escalate_when: The fix would require redesigning Planning autopilot or changing issue closure semantics beyond terminal outcome custody.
canonical_core.next_action: Run AW implement proof and publish the #2239 PR after validation.
proof_expectations: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_summary_cli.py -k "terminal_outcome_contract or completion_gate_claim_authorization" -q
uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -k "closeout_trust or closeout_claim_boundary" -q
uv run python scripts/run_agentic_workspace.py summary --target . --format json
uv run python scripts/run_agentic_workspace.py doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
uv run python scripts/run_agentic_workspace.py report --target . --section runtime_mirror_consistency --format json
completion_criteria: Completion gates expose a terminal_outcome_contract with DELIVERED, BLOCKED, USER_PAUSED, and CONTINUE states.
CONTINUE is selected whenever safe in-scope continuation remains and final_response_authorized is false.
Pseudo blockers such as context pressure, task size, one-slice-complete, successful compaction, and work-remains are named invalid blockers.
Closeout compact reports and final-response rendering expose the contract so terminal final responses are blocked in CONTINUE.
Focused CLI, summary, lint, and generated-package freshness checks pass.
canonical_core.continuation_owner: none for #2239 after PR publication; remaining P0 issues continue in their own stacked branches.

goal: Close #2239 by making closeout custody explicit: DELIVERED, BLOCKED, USER_PAUSED, and CONTINUE are projected from completion gates, and CONTINUE forbids terminal final-response claims while safe in-scope continuation remains.
non_goals: Do not rewrite Planning autopilot end to end in this slice.
Do not close unrelated P0 issues from the #2239 PR.

invariants: CONTINUE never authorizes a terminal final response.
BLOCKED requires a typed external/human blocker and no safe continuation.
Progress updates do not transfer execution custody to the user.

durable_residue.learned constraint: Lazy report sections currently use workspace_runtime_core while direct helpers also exist in workspace_runtime_primitives; closeout semantics must be mirrored until that duplicate path is retired.
durable_residue.motivation worth preserving: Avoid future projection drift where direct unit tests pass but CLI lazy sections use stale core behavior.
durable_residue.promotion trigger: When runtime duplication cleanup is selected.
durable_residue.retention after promotion: retain until follow-up exists or duplication is removed

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.
closure_check.why this decision is honest: The issue-scoped behavior is implemented and focused proof passed; PR publication and CI remain the release step.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_summary_cli.py -k "terminal_outcome_contract or completion_gate_claim_authorization" -q; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -k "closeout_trust or closeout_claim_boundary" -q; uv run ruff check src\agentic_workspace\workspace_runtime_core.py src\agentic_workspace\workspace_runtime_primitives.py src\agentic_workspace\workspace_runtime_proof.py src\agentic_workspace\reporting_support.py tests\test_workspace_cli.py tests\test_workspace_summary_cli.py; uv run python scripts\generate\generate_command_packages.py --check
closure_check.reopen trigger: Review or CI shows CONTINUE can still authorize terminal final responses or BLOCKED is used for pseudo blockers.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, report AW smoothness/helpfulness gaps, better-way signals, unused-feature reflections, and places AW could help more. Route each concrete signal to exactly one owner class unless explicitly split, or mark no_signal_found after checking.
signals found: workspace_runtime_core/workspace_runtime_primitives duplicate closeout path caused initial CLI projection drift
signals fixed: Mirrored #2239 terminal outcome wiring into both runtime copies for this issue
signals routed: Durable cleanup candidate: retire or generate-check the duplicated closeout runtime path
